case_summary: A complaint has been filed with the Haryana Police regarding an incident related to the Haryana Gauvansh Sanrakshan and Gausamvardhan Act, 2015. The complainant, Udham Singh, has reported an occurrence of an offence under this Act.

sections.title: Theft
sections.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to take it, is said to commit theft.
sections.relevance: Not directly relevant to the case at hand, as the primary complaint is regarding the Haryana Gauvansh Sanrakshan and Gausamvardhan Act, 2015.
sections.punishment: As per the context, not directly applicable.

sop.action: Register FIR immediately under BNSS Section 173

sc_judgments.case_name: Lalita Kumari vs Govt. of UP (2014)
sc_judgments.guideline: FIR must be registered immediately on receipt of cognizable offense
sc_judgments.holding: The Constitution Bench unanimously held that registration of FIR is mandatory under Section 154 CrPC (now BNSS 173) if the information discloses commission of a cognizable offence.
key_points: FIR cannot be refused on any pretext for cognizable offences
Preliminary inquiry can only be done in cases like matrimonial disputes, commercial cases, medical negligence, corruption - not in serious crimes
Officer refusing to register FIR is liable under departmental action and contempt of court
Victim can approach SP or Magistrate if SHO refuses
sc_judgments.io_duty: Mandatory - Register FIR immediately. No preliminary inquiry for cognizable offences. Non-compliance invites disciplinary action.

special_laws.act_name: The Haryana Gauvansh Sanrakshan and Gausamvardhan Act, 2015
special_laws.action: Investigate the complaint under the relevant sections of the Act.

evidence_checklist: Seize any relevant documents or evidence related to the Haryana Gauvansh Sanrakshan and Gausamvardhan Act, 2015
Record witness statements under BNSS Sec 180
io_warnings: Ensure that the complaint is investigated thoroughly and that all relevant evidence is collected and preserved.
